visual_dna.skin_tone: warm olive, smooth, young in early scenes
visual_dna.hair_texture: straight to wavy — always covered by headscarf in public scenes
visual_dna.eye_color: dark brown — deep and reflective, she 'pondered these things in her heart'
visual_dna.height_build: petite, graceful — young girl in annunciation scenes
distinguishing_marks: head covering always present in public — culturally accurate
quiet strength in facial expression — not passive, chosen
expression shifts from girl to mother across Luke's narrative

wardrobe_by_arc.annunciation: simple blue-grey linen headscarf and modest tunic — working girl's clothes, Nazareth interior
wardrobe_by_arc.visitation: travel robe over tunic, slightly dusty from journey to hill country
wardrobe_by_arc.nativity: travel-worn dark blue outer garment, exhausted but luminous
wardrobe_by_arc.temple_presentation: clean ceremonial dress — modest but their best for the Temple
wardrobe_by_arc.passion: dark mourning tones, tear-stained, grief-aged face of a woman in her 40s

voice_profile.register: warm mezzo-soprano, soft — rises to prophetic when the Spirit fills her
voice_profile.pace: careful, thoughtful — she speaks rarely but each word is weighted
voice_profile.accent_note: Galilean Aramaic, young woman's voice in early scenes
speech_mannerisms: the Magnificat — prophetic declaration, not a lullaby
questions that show she is thinking deeply: 'How will this be?'
stores words rather than speaks them — 'Mary treasured all these things'
'Do whatever he tells you' — her last recorded words, at Cana
voice_profile.notes: The Magnificat (Luke 1:46-55) must not be softened. She is prophesying justice and the overturning of power.

emotional_arc.opening: Wonder mixed with fear at annunciation — then courage: 'Let it be to me according to your word.' The most consequential yes in history from a teenage girl.
emotional_arc.midpoint: The sword Simeon prophesies piercing her soul — she carries knowledge of what's coming through years of watching her son's ministry.
emotional_arc.climax: At the foot of the cross — not running, not collapsing. Standing. The most costly thing she ever did.
emotional_arc.resolution: Present in the upper room at Pentecost (Acts 1). The grief held. The faith completed.

evangelical_baptist.notes: The obedient servant, handmaid of the Lord — her yes makes the gospel possible
evangelical_baptist.marketing_angle: Before the manger, there was her answer. 'Let it be to me.' Everything started there.

catholic.notes: The Blessed Mother, Theotokos — Queen of Heaven, model of faith, intercessor
catholic.marketing_angle: The Blessed Mother — her courage is the beginning of the whole story

black_church.notes: A young woman of color under occupation, saying yes to God at enormous personal cost
black_church.marketing_angle: A teenager under Roman occupation who trusted God with everything — and meant it

hispanic_christian.notes: La Virgen — central to Hispanic Catholic and Pentecostal faith, deeply personal
hispanic_christian.marketing_angle: La Virgen — her courage is our courage. Her yes changed everything.

secular_curious.notes: A teenage girl who said yes to something that would cost her everything — historically extraordinary
secular_curious.marketing_angle: A teenager who changed the world without ever holding a sword or a crown

youth_gen_z.notes: Young, overlooked by the world, chosen by God — her story is one of radical trust
youth_gen_z.marketing_angle: Fourteen years old. Nobody. And she said yes to the most dangerous thing God ever asked.

jewish_american.notes: Miriam — a young Jewish woman, her Magnificat echoes Hannah's prayer and the prophets
jewish_american.marketing_angle: Miriam of Nazareth — her song echoes Hannah, Deborah, the prophets. Deeply Jewish.

women.notes: The most important woman in the western tradition — told on her own terms
women.marketing_angle: She was not a background figure. She was the first believer. The last at the cross.

men.notes: The mother who raised him — her courage grounds the nativity
men.marketing_angle: The woman who stood at the cross while most of the men had run

audience_targeting.modern_parallel: The young woman who said yes to something that would cost her everything — and meant it
audience_targeting.modern_parallel_detail: Not a passive figure. She asks hard questions (How will this be?), she prophesies justice (Magnificat), she stands at the cross when others flee.

ai_generation.consistency_seed_prompt: 1st century Jewish young woman age 14-16, warm olive skin, dark hair under blue-grey linen headscarf, dark reflective brown eyes, petite graceful build, simple modest linen tunic, Galilean village interior, oil lamp warm light, expression of wonder and courage, cinematic photoreal, soft natural light, 8k, film grain
ai_generation.negative_prompt: European features, blonde hair, blue eyes, crown, halo, cartoonish, painted, white skin, anachronistic clothing, sexualized depiction, glamorous styling
ai_generation.midjourney_style_params: cinematic photoreal, soft diffused natural light, intimate framing, warm earth tones
ai_generation.firefly_prompt: Photorealistic portrait of a 1st century Jewish teenage girl, age 14-16, warm olive complexion, dark hair covered by a simple blue-grey linen headscarf, dark expressive brown eyes, petite build, modest linen tunic, Galilean home interior, warm oil lamp light, expression shifting from fear to quiet courage, intimate cinematic framing
reference_films: The Nativity Story (2006) — Keisha Castle-Hughes casting, authentic age and ethnicity
Young Woman and the Sea (2024) — period authenticity, young female lead emotional realism
ai_generation.color_grading_notes: Annunciation: warm amber oil-lamp tones. Nativity: cool blue night with warm lamp center. Passion: desaturated grief-blues.

production_notes: Critical: cast Mary at historically accurate age (14-16) for annunciation/nativity scenes. Do not age up.
